hypothesis: Fine-tuning the pre-trained eSEN model on a modest DFT dataset of ~200 SSNEB images sampled from CsPbI3 β→γ and α→γ transition pathways using a multi-task loss that weights energy:force:stress equally (1:1:1) will reduce the number of VASP SSNEB restart iterations required to converge the MEP by at least 30% and prevent the ML-induced lattice symmetry breaking seen with the un-finetuned model.
